Background:

{A) ATFCITC is registered with the DoT as an Infrastructure Provider Category-| (IP-t) for
providing passive telecom infrastructure services and owns or has acquired and
possesses, or will acquire and possess, certain telecommuniations sites,
infrastructure and equipment in various Telecom Circles in India and is engaged, inter
alia, in providing Passive Infrastructure (as defined below) and related operations and
maintenance services to, amongst others various telecommunications Service
Provider / operators in India on a shared basis.

{B) in order to comply with the Government of India's directives and objectives, in
particular to encourage extensive infrastructure sharing, Airtel proposes to create a
high quality, rapid, wide coverage mobile telecommunications network in India that
takes advantage of shared passive infrastructure.

(C) Accordingly, the Airtel wishes to access and share certain Passive Infrastructure
provided by ATCITC or to be provided by ATCITC and to do all acts necessary for
instalation of Active Infrastructure, to the extent permitted by applicable laws in India.

(D} ATCITC is willing to offer its Passive Infrastructure and share and allow access to its
equipment for use by the Airtel to ine extent permitted by applicable laws in india and,
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises, the mutual covenants, terms
and conditions and understandings set forth herein, the Parties with the intent to be
legally bound hereby covenant and agree as follows

1 Definitions and Interpretation
1.1 Definitions |

In this Agreement, the following terms shall have the following meanings unless the
context otherwise requires:

*Additiona! User” means any user of a site other than Airtel

‘Active Infrastructure” includes base terminal station equipment, associated

site other than Passive infrastructure (as defined below);

"Affected Party” has the meaning given to that term in Ciause 16.1;

“Approved Contractor” means a person or team of persons (including the Airtel and
its employees) approved by infrastructure Provider in accordance with the applicable

which it is a Subsidiary (its “Holding Company’) and any other Subsidiaries of such

“Insurance Policies” has the meaning given to that term in Clause 15;

PERMITTED USE

Airtel shall be entitled to use the Communications Facilities (including the Tower and any
additions thereto) at each of the Sites for the purpose of installing and operating
Communications Equipment in accordance with the terms and conditions as stipulated in this
Agreement. Subject to approval in writing by ATCITC and Mutually agreed payment, Airtel
shall also be entitled to the placement of signage at each of the Sites at a designated place.
signage size shall not exceed 2’x4' provided that such placement does not impair ATCITC’s
ability to market the Sites to Additional Users and such Additional User's ability to utilize the
Sites. All statutory levies related to this signage shaljl be borne by respective users.

ATCITC may approach Additional Users and such Additional Users shali be entitled to use
the Communications Facilities (including the Tower and any additions theretc) at each of the
Sites for the purpose of operating Communications Equipment and ATCITC shall only permit
the use of the Site for such purposes. Any use of active electronics will be at the sole
discretion of Airtel and it will not be négotiable. Scheme of active electrenics shall be mutually
discussed between ATCiTC and Airtel. Physical occupancy limits are to be predefined and
agreed as far as power, space in shelter and space in tower.

The use of the Communications Facilities at each of the Sites shall be subject to the Leave
and License Agreement (“Site Agreement"), that has been entered with respect to each Site.

Airtel shalt have the right, at Airtel’s cost and expense, to alter, replace, modify and
expand any Site, as Aiftel deems reasonably necessary or desirable pursuant to this
Agreement, provided, however, that such aiterations, replacements, modifications or
expansions (“Airtel Improvements”) are permitted under the applicable Site
Agreement or prior consent of ATCITC concemed has been obtained in this regard, as
the case may be, and are performed in campliance with the Applicable Laws; provided
that such Airtel improvements shall remain the sole property of Airtel and not be utilized
by any Additional User, Airtel would not be required to pay any Additional Monthly Fee
for equipment located on/ in Airtel Improvements.

4.2 Modification etc. of Statutes
References to a statute or statutory provision include:

1.24 that statute or provision as from time to time modified, re-enacted or
consolidated whether before or after the date of this Agreement:

1.22 any past statute or statutory provision (as from time to time modified, re-
enacted or consolidated) which that statute or provision has directly or
indirectly replaced; and

1.2.3 any subordinate legislation made from time to time under that statute or
Statutory provision which is in force at the date of this Agreement.

1.3 Singular, Plural, Gender

References to one gender include all genders and references to the singular include
the plural and vice versa.

1.4 References to Persons and Companies

References to:

1.4.1. @ person include any company, partnership or unincorporated association
{whether or not having separate legal personality): and

1.4.2  acompany shalt include any company, corporation or any body corporate,
wherever incorporated.

1.5 Schedules etc,

References to this Agreement shall include any Recitals and Schedules to it and
references to Clauses and Schedules are to Clauses of, and Schedules to, this
Agreement. References to paragraphs in a Schedule are references to paragraphs in
that Schedule.

1.6 Headings

Headings shall be ignored in interpreting this Agreement.

2 Site Access
2.1 Provision of Passive Infrastructure

2.1.1 ATCITC shall provide Site Access Availability to Airtel in accordance with
the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

2.1.2 Throughout the Term of this Agreement, Airtel shail be entitled to provide
notice to ATCITC of those Sites in relation to which it wishes to be granted
Site Access Availability (a “Service Order’). The process for issuing a
Service Order shall be as specified in Schedule 1 (Site Access
Avaitability).

2.1.3 ATCITC shail ensure that each Site is capable of accommodating Airtel
Equipment in accordance with the standard configuration set out in
Paragraph 1 of Schedule 1 (Site Access Availability), Any additional
requirements shall be specified by Airtel in the Service Order.

2.1.4 Inthe event that the Service Orders received by ATCITC in respect of any
Site(s) mean that the available Passive Infrastructure at such Site(s) are
over-subscribed, an applicant whose Service Order was received by
ATCITC prior to another Service Order shall be given priority by ATCITC
while allocating such Passive Infrastructure to the relevant applicants.

2.1.5 With respect to each Site in relation to which ATCITC is able to grant Site
Access Availability, the Parties shall execute a Service Contract in
accordance with the procedure set out in Schedule 1 (Site Access
Availability}, and the provisions of each Service Contract shall include the
standard terms set out in Schedule 5 (Standard Site Access Terms).
Each Service Contract shall be duly stamped and the applicabie stamp
duty shall be at the Airtel’s expense.

2.1.6 Upon the execution of a Service Contract in respect of a Site, Airtel shall
have the right to install the Airtel Equipment or any portion thereof at such
Site. Airtel shall have access to each such Site for ail installation
activities and ATCITC shail provide to Airtel the necessary means of
access for the said purposes, of ingress and egress from each such Site
In accordance with the terms of the Service Contract. Provided, however,
that only authorized employees of Airtel or its properly authorized sub-
contractors shall be allowed such access to the Sites.

2.4.7 The right, title and interest in and to the Passive Infrastructure, including
any enhancements carried out by ATCITC, shali vest with ATCITC and all
such enhancements thereto shall be at the sole cost and expense of
ATCIFC. Enhancements in this context means the augmentation in
Capacity carried out by ATCITC to achieve increased sharing. In case of
any enhancements that are not covered in the Scope of this Agreement
and are made on specific requisition from the Operator, the commercials
for the same would be mutually agreed to between the Operator and
ATCITC.

2.2 Standard of Site Access Availiability

2.2.1 Site Access Availability at any Site shail meet the relevant Site Access
Service Levels.

2.2.2 ATCITC shall ensure that it puts in place processes and procedures to
monitor its performance against the Site Access Service Levels and shail
report such information te Airtel on a monthly basis.

Zid Site Access Service Credits

2.3.1 If ATCITC faits to provide the Site Access Availability in accordance with
the Site Access Service Levels then Site Access Service Credits shall
become payable in accordance with Schedule 1 (Site Access Availability}.
Airtel may, on a quarterly basis and with a notice of 30 days, deduct any
such Site Access Service Credits from the next invoice due under this
Agreement or, if no further invoices are due, may recover the Site Access
Service Credits as a debt from ATCITC due and payable within 30 days of
demand.

2.3.2 The Parties agree that the Site Access Service Credits are an estimate of
the adjustment to the Charges to reflect the value of services actually
rendered and are not a penaity, and are, at the amounts stated in this
Agreement, reasonable.

2.4 Service Failures

2.4.4 Without prejudice to any other obligations of ATCITC under this
Agreement, ATCITC shall promptly warm Ajrtel whenever it has
reasonable grounds to believe that any development (including failure on
the part of ATCITC to carry out its obligations and responsibilities under
this Agreement) will have, or threatens to have, a detrimental effect on the
Site Access Availability. ATCITC shall take all reasonable steps to prevent
such development from occurring and to prevent its reoccurrence.

2.4.2 If ATCITC fails to provide the Site Access Availability in accordance with
this Agreement then, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies it
may have under this Agreement or otherwise, Airtel may require ATCITC
by notice in writing to carry out an investigation into the cause of such
failure. ATCITC shall carry out such an investigation at its own cost and
shall provide a report of that investigation to Airtel.

2.5 Non-Exclusive

ATCITC retains the right to provide Site Access Availability to other
telecommunications operators and Aijrte!l retains the right to seek passive
infrastructure services from other passive infrastructure providers.

2.6 Approvals

2.6.4 ATCITC shall be responsible for obtaining of all necessary and requisite
Permits for the installation of any Passive Infrastructure, including, without
limitation, municipal and local bodies approvals, electricity board

convenient time for such ATCITC Downtime, ATCITC shall plan such ATCITC Downtimes
between 1:00 am and 5:00 am. Prior to the commencement of any planned ATCITC
